article_meta.title: Venezuelan Political Crisis Venezuela releases multiple American citizens from prison following military operation At le

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Venezuelan Political Crisis Venezuela releases multiple American citizens from prison following military operation At le
SOURCE: Brit Hume

ARTICLE:

Venezuelan Political Crisis Venezuela releases multiple American citizens from prison following military operation At least four Americans were freed by Venezuela's interim government in the first known release since a US military operation captured President Nicolás Maduro By Michael Sinkewicz Fox News Published January 13, 2026 10:55pm EST s Flipboard Comments Print Email Add Fox News on Google close Video NEW: Americans urged to LEAVE Venezuela amid security concerns Fox News correspondent Madeleine Rivera shares details on the State Department's plea for U.S. citizens to leave Venezuela amid a transition of power following the capture of Nicolás Maduro. The interim government in Venezuela has released at least four U.S. citizens who were imprisoned under President Nicolás Maduro's regime, Fox News confirmed. The release marks the first known release of Americans in the South American country since the U.S. military completed an operation to capture Maduro, who is now facing federal drug trafficking charges in New York."We welcome the release of detained Americans in Venezuela," a State Department official said Tuesday. "This is an important step in the right direction by the interim authorities." TRUMP SIGNS ORDER TO PROTECT VENEZUELA OIL REVENUE HELD IN US ACCOUNTS Venezuelans celebrate after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro had been captured and flown out of the country in Santiago, Chile, Saturday, Jan. 3, 2026. (Esteban Felix/AP Photo) Venezuela's interim government has reported that 116 prisoners have been released, although only about 70 have been verified by the non-governmental organization Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón, according to Bloomberg.National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez said prisoner releases would continue, according to the outlet.
---

```json
{
  "summary": "Venezuela's interim government has released at least four American citizens from prison, marking the first known release since a U.S. military operation captured President Nicolás Maduro. The former president now faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York. A State Department official welcomed the releases as \"an important step in the right direction by the interim authorities.\" The interim government reports releasing 116 prisoners total, though only approximately 70 have been verified by the non-governmental organization Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón. National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez indicated that additional prisoner releases would continue. The State Department has urged American citizens to leave Venezuela amid security concerns during the country's transition of power. President Trump has also signed an order to protect Venezuelan oil revenue held in U.S. accounts during this period of political transition.",
  "key_facts": [
    "At least four American citizens were released from Venezuelan prisons by the interim government",
    "The releases are the first known since a U.S. military operation captured President Nicolás Maduro",
    "Maduro now faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York",
    "Venezuela's interim government reports releasing 116 prisoners total, with approximately 70 verified by NGO Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón",
    "The U.S. State Department has urged American citizens to leave Venezuela amid the political transition"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "U.S. State Department",
    "Fox News reporting",
    "Venezuelan interim government/National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez",
    "Bloomberg",
    "Non-governmental organization Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: Venezuela's interim government has released at least four American citizens from prison, marking the first known release since a U.S. military operation captured President Nicolás Maduro. The former president now faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York. A State Department official welcomed the releases as "an important step in the right direction by the interim authorities." The interim government reports releasing 116 prisoners total, though only approximately 70 have been verified by the non-governmental organization Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón. National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez indicated that additional prisoner releases would continue. The State Department has urged American citizens to leave Venezuela amid security concerns during the country's transition of power. President Trump has also signed an order to protect Venezuelan oil revenue held in U.S. accounts during this period of political transition.
key_facts: At least four American citizens were released from Venezuelan prisons by the interim government
The releases are the first known since a U.S. military operation captured President Nicolás Maduro
Maduro now faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York
Venezuela's interim government reports releasing 116 prisoners total, with approximately 70 verified by NGO Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón
The U.S. State Department has urged American citizens to leave Venezuela amid the political transition
sources_represented: Venezuelan interim government/National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez
Non-governmental organization Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón
